message:
feat(agentmail): implement Human Overseer messaging for ntm mail

Add the ability for human operators to send high-priority messages to AI
agents via the Agent Mail Human Overseer functionality. This provides a
CLI interface for the overseer endpoint that:

- Sends from the special "HumanOverseer" identity
- Bypasses contact policies (humans can always reach any agent)
- Auto-injects a preamble telling agents to prioritize instructions
- Marks all messages as high importance automatically

Implementation details:

agentmail/client.go:
- httpBaseURL(): Derives HTTP REST API base URL from MCP endpoint
- ProjectSlugFromPath(): Computes project slug from absolute path

agentmail/tools.go:
- SendOverseerMessage(): HTTP POST to /mail/{project}/overseer/send
- ListProjectAgents(): Retrieve registered agents for recipient discovery

agentmail/types.go:
- OverseerMessageOptions: Recipients, subject, body, optional thread ID
- OverseerSendResult: Success status, message ID, recipients, timestamp

cli/mail.go:
- Complete refactor from agent-to-agent to human-to-agent messaging
- Opens $EDITOR for message composition when no body provided
- Subject auto-derivation from first markdown heading or body truncation
- --all flag sends to all registered agents in project
- Clearer error messages with Agent Mail server startup hints

This is the CLI equivalent of the Agent Mail web UI at:
/mail/{project}/overseer/compose

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"ntm-7a9","title":"Implement model-based filtering in send command","description":"# Implement Model-Based Filtering in send Command

## Purpose

Allow targeting specific models when sending prompts.

## Current Syntax

```bash
ntm send myproject --cc "message"     # All Claude agents
ntm send myproject --all "message"    # All agents
```

## Enhanced Syntax

```bash
ntm send myproject --cc:opus "message"     # Only Opus agents
ntm send myproject --cc:sonnet "message"   # Only Sonnet agents
ntm send myproject --cod:gpt4 "message"    # Only GPT-4 agents
ntm send myproject --cc:opus --cc:sonnet "message"  # Opus + Sonnet
```

## Flag Parsing

Parse colon-separated type:model:
```go
type SendTarget struct {
    Type  AgentType
    Model string  // Empty = all models of type
}

func ParseSendTarget(flag string) SendTarget {
    parts := strings.SplitN(flag, ":", 2)
    target := SendTarget{Type: AgentType(parts[0])}
    if len(parts) > 1 {
        target.Model = parts[1]
    }
    return target
}
```

## Pane Matching

```go
func MatchesSendTarget(pane Pane, target SendTarget) bool {
    if pane.Type != target.Type {
        return false
    }
    if target.Model != "" && pane.Model != target.Model {
        return false
    }
    return true
}
```

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] --cc:model syntax parsed correctly
- [ ] Multiple model filters can be combined
- [ ] Works with panes using new naming format
- [ ] Error if model filter matches no panes
- [ ] Backwards compatible with --cc (no model)","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-10T03:21:45.479384-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-10T03:21:45.479384-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ntm-7a9","depends_on_id":"ntm-z2k","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-10T03:21:54.061286-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}

 {"id":"ntm-7lk","title":"SUB-EPIC: Integration Tests","description":"# SUB-EPIC: Integration Tests

## Scope
Integration tests that verify component interactions across package boundaries.

## Key Integration Points

### 1. CLI → tmux Integration
- spawn command creates real sessions with correct pane layout
- send command delivers prompts to correct panes
- interrupt command sends Ctrl+C to all agent panes
- status command reports accurate pane states

### 2. CLI → config Integration
- Commands load config from correct locations
- Config changes affect command behavior
- Default values work correctly

### 3. robot → tmux Integration
- --robot-status outputs accurate session info
- --robot-plan suggests valid actions
- JSON output is parseable and accurate

### 4. status → tmux Integration
- Agent state detection works with real pane output
- Error detection triggers correctly
- Prompt detection works across agent types

### 5. palette → config Integration
- Palette loads commands from config
- Custom palette files are honored
- Categories are properly organized

### 6. output → cli Integration
- --json flag enables JSON output across commands
- Text output formatting is consistent
- Error output goes to correct stream

## Test Approach
- Use real tmux sessions (cleanup on test end)
- Use temp directories for config
- Capture and verify actual output
- Test error conditions explicitly

## Prerequisites
- Unit tests for individual packages should pass first
- tmux must be installed on test system","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2025-12-10T03:18:50.889846-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-10T03:18:50.889846-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ntm-7lk","depends_on_id":"ntm-l5h","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-10T03:19:05.947564-05:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"ntm-7lk","depends_on_id":"ntm-idx","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-10T03:19:06.114595-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}

 {"id":"ntm-7nk3","title":"EPIC: Checkpoint & Rollback System","description":"# Checkpoint & Rollback System

## Background & Motivation

AI agents sometimes go off track. By the time users notice, significant work may be wasted.

Checkpoints provide quick recovery:
1. Create named save points before risky operations
2. Roll back to known-good state when things go wrong

## What Gets Checkpointed

### Captured (for restoration)
- **Git state**: Uncommitted changes saved as patch
- **Session layout**: Pane configuration, agent types

### Captured (for reference only)
- **Pane scrollback**: What agents were doing (NOT restored)
- **Timestamp and description**: Human context

### NOT Captured
- Agent internal state (we can't access it)
- API conversation history (we can't access it)

## IMPORTANT: What Rollback Does

Rollback restores GIT STATE, not agent state.

```bash
ntm rollback myproject "before-refactor"
```

This will:
1. ✅ Stash current uncommitted changes
2. ✅ Apply checkpoint's git patch
3. ✅ Interrupt all agents (Ctrl+C)
4. ⚠️ Agents restart with FRESH context (no memory of pre-rollback work)

**Critical understanding**: Agents don't "remember" the checkpoint. They start fresh. The checkpoint restores FILES, not agent memory.

## Command Interface

```bash
# Create checkpoint
ntm checkpoint myproject "before-refactor"
# Captures: git diff, session layout, scrollback (for reference)

# List checkpoints
ntm checkpoints myproject
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│ Checkpoints: myproject                                      │
├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│ before-refactor    │ 15 min ago │ 3 files │ 4 agents      │
│ api-working        │ 2 hrs ago  │ 1 file  │ 4 agents      │
│ auto-20251210-1400 │ 3 hrs ago  │ 8 files │ 4 agents      │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘

# Preview rollback
ntm rollback myproject "before-refactor" --dry-run

# Execute rollback
ntm rollback myproject "before-refactor"
# Warning: This will reset files and interrupt agents.
# Current changes will be stashed. Continue? [y/N]

# Quick rollback to last checkpoint
ntm rollback myproject --last
```

## Storage Format

```
~/.config/ntm/checkpoints/myproject/before-refactor/
├── metadata.json         # Timestamp, description, session info
├── git-state.patch       # git diff output
├── session-layout.json   # Pane configuration
└── scrollback/           # Reference only (not restored)
    ├── cc_1.txt
    └── cod_1.txt
```

## Auto-Checkpoints

Configurable automatic checkpoints:
```toml
[checkpoints]
auto_enabled = true
auto_events = ["broadcast_send"]  # Before ntm send --all
max_auto_checkpoints = 10
retention_days = 7
```

## Validation

Before rollback:
- Check if checkpoint exists
- Warn if checkpoint is old (>24h)
- Warn if git state has diverged significantly
- Validate patch can apply

## Success Criteria

- [ ] ntm checkpoint creates named checkpoints
- [ ] Git state (uncommitted) captured as patch
- [ ] Session layout captured for reference
- [ ] Scrollback captured for reference (not restored)
- [ ] ntm rollback restores git state
- [ ] Agents interrupted on rollback (fresh start)
- [ ] Auto-checkpoint on configurable events
- [ ] Retention policy with rotation
- [ ] Clear user messaging about what rollback does/doesn't do","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2025-12-10T03:28:29.179411-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-10T03:36:34.909996-05:00"}

@@ -195,3 +195,21 @@ type SearchResult struct {
 	ThreadID    *string   `json:"thread_id"`
 	From        string    `json:"from"`
 }
+
+// OverseerMessageOptions contains options for sending a Human Overseer message.
+// Human Overseer messages bypass contact policies and are auto-marked as high importance.
+type OverseerMessageOptions struct {
+	ProjectSlug string   // Project slug (derived from project path)
+	Recipients  []string // Agent names to send to
+	Subject     string   // Subject line (max 200 chars)
+	BodyMD      string   // Markdown body (max 49,600 chars)
+	ThreadID    string   // Optional thread ID for conversation continuity
+}
+
+// OverseerSendResult contains the result of sending a Human Overseer message.
+type OverseerSendResult struct {
+	Success    bool      `json:"success"`
+	MessageID  int       `json:"message_id"`
+	Recipients []string  `json:"recipients"`
+	SentAt     time.Time `json:"sent_at"`
+}
